heading: Tax on certain dispositions by employee stock ownership plans and certain cooperatives
label: 4978 Tax on certain dispositions by employee stock ownership plans and certain cooperatives
notes: Amendments2004—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “(60 percent” for “60 percent”.Pub. L. 108–3111997—Subsec. (a). , inserted “or acquired any qualified employer securities in a qualified gratuitous transfer to which section 664(g) applied” after “section 1042 applied” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(11)(A)Subsec. (a)(2). , inserted before comma at end “60 percent of the total value of all employer securities as of such disposition in the case of any qualified employer securities acquired in a qualified gratuitous transfer to which section 664(g) applied)”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(11)(B)Subsec. (b)(2). , inserted “or acquired in the qualified gratuitous transfer to which section 664(g) applied” after “section 1042 applied” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(12)(A)Subsec. (b)(2)(A). , inserted “or to which section 664(g) applied” after “section 1042 applied”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(12)(B)Subsec. (c). , substituted “written statement described in section 664(g)(1)(E) or in section 1042(b)(3) (as the case may be)” for “written statement described in section 1042(b)(3)”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(13)Subsec. (e)(2). , inserted before period at end “; except that such section shall be applied without regard to subparagraph (B) thereof for purposes of applying this section and section 4979A with respect to securities acquired in a qualified gratuitous transfer (as defined in section 664(g)(1))”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 1530(c)(14)1996—Subsec. (b)(2). added subpars. (A) and (B) and closing provisions and struck out former subpars. (A) to (D) and closing provisions which read as follows:Pub. L. 104–188“(A) first, from section 133 securities (as defined in section 4978B(e)(2)) acquired during the 3-year period ending on the date of such disposition, beginning with the securities first so acquired.“(B) second, from section 133 securities (as so defined) acquired before such 3-year period unless such securities (or proceeds from the disposition) have been allocated to accounts of participants or beneficiaries.“(C) third, from qualified securities to which section 1042 applied acquired during the 3-year period ending on the date of the disposition, beginning with the securities first so acquired, and“(D) then from any other employer securities.If subsection (d) or section 4978B(d) applies to a disposition, the disposition shall be treated as made from employer securities in the opposite order of the preceding sentence.”1989—Subsec. (b)(2). substituted “determined as if such securities were disposed of—”, subpars. (A) to (D), and concluding provision for “(determined as if such securities were disposed of in the order described in section 4978A(e))”.Pub. L. 101–2391988—Subsec. (d)(4). added par. (4).Pub. children.heading: Tax on dispositions of securities to which section 1042 applies before close of minimum holding period
children.label: a Tax on dispositions of securities to which section 1042 applies before close of minimum holding period

children.text: the total number of shares held by such plan or cooperative after such disposition is less than the total number of employer securities held immediately after such sale, or

except to the extent provided in regulations, the value of qualified securities held by such plan or cooperative after such disposition is less than 30 percent of the total value of all employer securities as of such disposition (60 percent of the total value of all employer securities as of such disposition in the case of any qualified employer securities acquired in a qualified gratuitous transfer to which section 664(g) applied),

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a) shall be equal to 10 percent of the amount realized on the disposition.

children.label: A
children.text: first from qualified securities to which section 1042 applied or to which section 664(g) applied acquired during the 3-year period ending on the date of the disposition, beginning with the securities first so acquired, and

children.label: B
children.text: then from any other employer securities.

children.heading: Distributions to employees
children.label: 3 Distributions to employees
children.text: The amount realized on any distribution to an employee for less than fair market value shall be determined as if the qualified security had been sold to the employee at fair market value.

children.heading: Liability for payment of taxes
children.label: c Liability for payment of taxes

children.text: the employer, or
the eligible worker-owned cooperative,

children.heading: Section not to apply to certain dispositions
children.label: d Section not to apply to certain dispositions
A
children.text: the death of the employee,

children.label: B
children.text: the retirement of the employee after the employee has attained 59½ years of age,

children.label: C
children.text: the disability of the employee (within the meaning of section 72(m)(7)), or

children.label: D
children.text: the separation of the employee from service for any period which results in a 1-year break in service (within the meaning of section 411(a)(6)(A)).

children.heading: Certain reorganizations
children.label: 2 Certain reorganizations
children.text: In the case of any exchange of qualified securities in any reorganization described in section 368(a)(1) for stock of another corporation, such exchange shall not be treated as a disposition for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Liquidation of corporation into cooperative
children.label: 3 Liquidation of corporation into cooperative
children.text: In the case of any exchange of qualified securities pursuant to the liquidation of the corporation issuing qualified securities into the eligible worker-owned cooperative in a transaction which meets the requirements of section 332 (determined by substituting “100 percent” for “80 percent” each place it appears in section 332(b)(1)), such exchange shall not be treated as a disposition for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Dispositions to meet diversification requirements
children.label: 4 Dispositions to meet diversification requirements
children.text: This section shall not apply to any disposition of qualified securities which is required under section 401(a)(28).

children.heading: Employee stock ownership plan
children.label: 1 Employee stock ownership plan
children.text: The term “employee stock ownership plan” has the meaning given to such term by section 4975(e)(7).

children.heading: Qualified securities
children.label: 2 Qualified securities
children.text: The term “qualified securities” has the meaning given to such term by section 1042(c)(1); except that such section shall be applied without regard to subparagraph (B) thereof for purposes of applying this section and section 4979A with respect to securities acquired in a qualified gratuitous transfer (as defined in section 664(g)(1)).

children.heading: Eligible worker-owned cooperative
children.label: 3 Eligible worker-owned cooperative
children.text: The term “eligible worker-owned cooperative” has the meaning given to such term by section 1042(c)(2).

children.heading: Disposition
children.label: 4 Disposition
children.text: The term “disposition” includes any distribution.

children.heading: Employer securities
children.label: 5 Employer securities
children.text: The term “employer securities” has the meaning given to such term by section 409().l
